title: HTTP JSON Extractor
description: Fetches JSON from an HTTP endpoint and writes it as a table. Supports a single request or incremental pagination.

url.description: The HTTP endpoint to fetch JSON from.
url.x-datuplet-doc: Required. Fetched with a plain GET request (no query-string templating beyond what pagination adds). Include any static query parameters directly in the URL.

array_path.description: Key of the response's top-level field holding the record array (omit for a bare JSON array response).
array_path.x-datuplet-doc: Only needed when the response is an object wrapping the array (e.g. {"results": [...]}) rather than a bare JSON array. If omitted and the response is an object, the component auto-detects the first array-valued field. Also used, in the fallback case below, as part of the output table name.

table_name.description: Output table name. Falls back to array_path, then "data", when omitted.
table_name.x-datuplet-doc: Resolution order applied by the component: table_name (if set) > array_path (if set) > the literal "data". Because that fallback is computed from two other fields, only the case where table_name is set directly can be resolved as a known upstream output by the pipeline builder's input picker (x-datuplet-produces points at this property); leaving it unset still works at runtime but the output table will show as an unresolvable dynamic name in the UI.

headers.description: HTTP request headers to send with the request(s).
headers.x-datuplet-doc: Plain string values — sent verbatim on every request, including every paginated page fetch. Use the $[name] secret-reference syntax for values that must not be stored in plaintext (e.g. an Authorization header).

pagination.description: Enables incremental, streaming pagination instead of a single request.
pagination.x-datuplet-doc: Pagination only activates when 'type' is set to a non-empty value; a pagination block present in config with 'type' omitted or empty is silently ignored in its entirety (the component falls back to a single-request fetch, discarding param/page_size/etc.) rather than failing — there is no config-validation error for this case.

type.description: Pagination style: incrementing page numbers, or incrementing row offsets.
type.x-datuplet-doc: Required to activate pagination at all (see the pagination-level note). "page" starts at 1 by default and increments by 1 per fetch; "offset" starts at 0 by default and increments by page_size per fetch, unless overridden by 'start'/'increment'.

param.description: Query parameter name carrying the current page/offset value.

start.x-datuplet-doc: When omitted (0), the component substitutes a type-dependent starting point: 1 for "page", 0 for "offset" (0 is already the zero-value for "offset", so this only visibly changes behaviour for "page").

increment.description: Amount added to the page/offset value after each fetch.
increment.x-datuplet-doc: When omitted (0), the component substitutes a type-dependent increment: page_size for "offset", 1 for "page" (and for any other/unrecognized type).

page_size.description: Expected number of results per page.
page_size.x-datuplet-doc: Used both as the default 'increment' for offset-style pagination and as a stop signal: a page returning fewer records than page_size is treated as the last page and pagination stops.

size_param.description: Query parameter name for the page-size value, if the API needs one sent explicitly.
size_param.x-datuplet-doc: Only sent when both size_param and page_size are set.

max_pages.description: Stop after fetching this many pages (0 = unlimited).
max_pages.x-datuplet-doc: Setting this to a non-zero value also disables the implicit stop_when_empty default (see stop_when_empty).

max_records.description: Stop after this many total records have been fetched (0 = unlimited).
max_records.x-datuplet-doc: The last page's records are truncated, not the whole run rejected, when this limit falls mid-page. Setting this to a non-zero value also disables the implicit stop_when_empty default (see stop_when_empty).

stop_when_empty.description: Stop pagination when a fetched page has zero records.
stop_when_empty.x-datuplet-doc: The runtime default is not a plain literal: if this is left false (the omitted/zero value) AND both max_pages and max_records are also left at 0 (unlimited), the component forces this on internally so pagination has some way to terminate. If either max_pages or max_records is set, an omitted stop_when_empty stays false — an empty page is not by itself a stop signal in that case.
